methods.name: getRowFromID
methods.description: Return the table row value, given a cell ID. *id - The cell id. Usually taken from panel values: celloverid, cellfocusid, etc.

methods.name: click
methods.signature: clickclick(row, col, clickCount=1, force=False, left=False, middle=False, right=False)
methods.description: Simulate a mouse click on a cell of the table. (0,0) is the top-left cell. *row, col - The row and column of the cell to click. *clickCount - (Keyword, Optional), Sets the number of clicks, for double clicking etc. *force - (Keyword, Optional) Forces the panel click, even if it's disabled. *left,middle,right - (Keyword, Optional) Override the default mouse button used. When none are set to true, the left mouse button is pressed, and the other buttons released.

methods.name: getColFromID
methods.description: Return the table column value, given a cell ID. *id - The cell id. Usually taken from panel values: celloverid, cellfocusid, etc.

methods.name: clickID
methods.signature: clickIDclickID(id, clickCount=1, force=False, left=False, middle=False, right=False)
methods.description: The same as the click method, except it uses a cell ID instead of the row/col to specify the cell to click.

methods.name: getCellID
methods.description: Return the cell ID value, given a row and column. *row, col - The table component row and column of interest.

methods.name: setKeyboardFocus
methods.description: Selects and sets the keyboard focus in a cell of the table if the cell is a field. *row, col - The row and column of the cell to set the keyboard focus. *selectAll - (Keyword, Optional) If True, then all text will be selected.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the TableCOMP Class wiki page
